title: Our world in photos: March 31
summary:
The Brooklyn Daily Eagle editorial staff highlights images from around the world, photos courtesy of the Associated Press.

title: 4 charged in corruption investigation linked to NYC homeless shelter operator
summary:
The indictment describes multiple layers of corruption within BHRAGS Home Care Corp., a nonprofit focused on home health care.

The post 4 charged in corruption investigation linked to NYC homeless shelter operator appeared first on Brooklyn Eagle.
author: Associated Press

title: Federal judge blocks Trump administration’s directive to cut funds from public broadcasting
summary:
THE TRUMP ADMINISTRATION’S EXECUTIVE ORDER TO END FEDERAL FUNDING for NPR and PBS is unlawful and unenforceable, a judge ruled.

The post Federal judge blocks Trump administration’s directive to cut funds from public broadcasting   appeared first on Brooklyn Eagle.
author: Brooklyn Eagle Staff

title: NYC launches first undergrad scholarship program for municipal workers
summary:
The new undergraduate scholarship program expands the longstanding Mayor’s Graduate Scholarship Program, which has run for 60 years.

The post NYC launches first undergrad scholarship program for municipal workers  appeared first on Brooklyn Eagle.
author: Brooklyn Eagle Staff

title: Police announce capture of alleged ‘ghost gun’ dealer
summary:
Matthew Streich of Gravesend is accused of using a 3D printer to produce at least eight illegal guns between December 2025 and February 2026.

The post Police announce capture of alleged ‘ghost gun’ dealer appeared first on Brooklyn Eagle.
author: Brooklyn Eagle Staff

summary:

title: Silver Alert issued for East Flatbush man last seen driving on Cross Bay Blvd.
summary:
Charles Moise left his residence around 5:30 p.m. Sunday, and was last seen in a white 2008 Ford Fusion bearing NY license plate #KMN1630.

title: Police seek suspects in Flatbush mugging
summary:
A 66-year-old woman was in front of 458 East 29 St. when two unidentified individuals approached her from behind and grabbed her purse.
